item.title: November 2025 Web Server Survey | Netcraft
item.description: In the November 2025 survey we received responses from 1,366,582,597 sites across 288,926,456 domains and 14,008,584 web-facing computers. This ref...

item.title: Fake Investment Platform Reputation Laundering: Felix Markets
item.description: Learn how Felix Markets posed as a regulated forex broker, exploited fake authorities, copied legal documents, and used sports sponsorship to appear legitimate.

item.title: Ultimate Fraud Prevention: How Banks Can Stop Scam Websites Fast
item.description: Discover how banks can detect, disrupt, and rapidly remove fake websites. Learn common spoofing tactics, fast takedown steps, and how Netcraft protects your customers.

item.title: Automated Threat Response at Scale: Netcraft Integrations & APIs
item.description: See how Netcraft integrations and APIs power faster detection, automated takedowns, and unified threat response across SIEMs, SOC tools, and internal systems.

item.title: Confluence “RFP” Phishing Scam Uses Weaponized SVGs to Steal Microsoft 365 Credentials
item.description: Attackers are abusing public Atlassian Confluence pages and weaponized SVG files to redirect victims to Microsoft 365 and Google phishing sites using advanced evasion techniques.

item.title: December 2025 Web Server Survey | Netcraft
item.description: In the December 2025 survey we received responses from 1,389,596,802 sites across 291,965,818 domains and 13,972,590 web-facing computers. This ref...

item.title: Lookalike Domains: Why “rn” vs “m” Still Works in Phishing
item.description: The “rn”→“m” homoglyph is still powering phishing via lookalike domains. See real examples across brands and how to detect and stop them.

item.title: Phishing Takedown Providers: A Definitive Guide to Speed, Automation, and Scale
item.description: Phishing is now industrialized. Learn how modern phishing takedown services use automation, AI, and global enforcement to stop impersonation attacks fast.

item.title: How to Prevent Phishing Attacks | Attack Recovery Guides
item.description: Phishing is a persistent threat many organizations face. Learn how to prevent phishing attacks on this helpful attack recovery guide from Netcraft.

item.title: How Scams Really Work: Inside the Industrial Fraud Ecosystem
item.description: With fraud losses hitting $12.5B in 2024, scams are more effective than ever. This deep dive explains the scam supply chain—and how to disrupt it at scale.

item.title: January 2026 Web Server Survey | Netcraft
item.description: In the January 2026 survey we received responses from 1,376,952,390 sites across 295,088,466 domains and 14,365,221 web-facing computers. This refl...

item.title: Part-Time Money Laundering: How Students Are Tricked into Becoming Money Mules
item.description: Criminals are targeting students with fake job offers that turn them into unwitting money mules. Learn how these scams work, the risks involved, and how Netcraft’s research helps expose them.

item.title: The Ultimate Guide to Domain Takedown Services in 2026
item.description: Learn how domain takedown services protect your brand from phishing, PhaaS, and smishing with rapid detection, automated blocking, and permanent content removal.

item.title: Netcraft | The Industry Leader in Enterprise Brand Protection
item.description: Leader in Phishing Detection, Cybercrime Disruption, and Website Takedown

item.title: Phone Scams Explained: How Criminal Call Centers Exploit Trust
item.description: Phone scams cost Americans $12.5B in 2024. Discover how organized fraud operations use spoofing, smishing, and psychological manipulation—and what actually stops them.

item.title: How to Monitor for Phishing Attacks Targeting Your Customers
item.description: Traditional anti-phishing is failing. Discover how to detect machine-speed deception and protect your brand's reputation from sophisticated AI phishing attacks.

item.title: February 2026 Web Server Survey | Netcraft
item.description: In the February 2026 survey we received responses from 1,419,977,535 sites across 297,071,903 domains and 14,379,914 web-facing computers. This ref...

item.title: Using Digital Risk Protection to Secure What EASM Can’t
item.description: Learn how digital risk protection (DRP) solutions uncover threats that external attack surface management (EASM) misses.

item.title: Remote Access Delivered Through Fake Zoom and Google Meet Calls
item.description: Netcraft uncovered a campaign using fake Zoom and Google Meet pages to lure victims into fraudulent meetings that deliver remote-access software. Learn how the attack works and how organizations can defend against it.

item.title: Middle East Crisis Exploited by Fraudsters: Government Impersonation and Evacuation Scam Infrastructure Identified
item.description: Threat actors are already exploiting the Middle East crisis with government-impersonation phishing and evacuation-themed domains. Netcraft investigates emerging fraud infrastructure and suspicious activity tied to the disruption.

item.title: The 5 Channels Digital Risk Protection Services Should Monitor
item.description: From fake apps to dark web leaks, learn the 5 critical channels a modern DRP service should monitor and why comprehensive coverage is essential in 2026.

item.title: Large-Scale Magento Defacement Campaign Impacts 15,000+ Sites
item.description: Netcraft uncovers a global Magento defacement campaign impacting 15,000+ hostnames across major brands, government domains, and e-commerce platforms. Learn how attackers are exploiting web infrastructure and what defenders should do next.

item.title: Preemptive Domain Disruption: Stopping Phishing Before It Starts
item.description: Netcraft introduces Preemptive Domain Disruption, using AI and Verified Attack Indicators to take down malicious infrastructure before attacks launch—eliminating the victimization window.

item.title: 5 Questions to Ask Your Next Digital Risk Protection Vendor
item.description: A DRP solution should reveal whether the platform actively disrupts attacks or simply reports them. Ask these five questions to find the right DRP vendor.

item.title: Excessive Emojis as an AI Indicator
item.description: See how "emoji clues" in code comments and console outputs serve as a indicator for identifying AI-generated phishing scripts and automated threat infrastructure.

item.title: March 2026 Web Server Survey | Netcraft
item.description: In the March 2026 survey we received responses from 1,427,812,919 sites across 297,642,950 domains and 14,225,050 web-facing computers. This reflec...

item.title: Ultimate Guide to Digital Risk Protection | Netcraft
item.description: Digital risk protection is no longer a niche function inside cybersecurity - learn what it is, why it matters, and how to stop brand impersonation, phishing, and online threats.

item.title: Phishing After Passkeys: What Attacks to Expect
item.description: Passkeys are phishing-resistant, but many deployments remain exposed during the transition from passwords. Netcraft explains the attack paths organisations should expect and how to reduce risk.

item.title: Ultimate RFP Guide for Digital Risk Protection
item.description: This cybersecurity procurement guide outlines how to structure an RFP process to find the best digital risk protection vendor, and includes a downloadable vendor questionnaire.

item.title: A Decade of Proactive Protection: Netcraft & NCSC Partnership
item.description: Explore how Netcraft and the UK’s NCSC have reduced phishing in the UK by up to 80% over a decade—through rapid takedowns, proactive detection, and ACD 2.0 innovation

item.title: Domain Protection Strategies | A Technical Guide
item.description: Threat actors continue to evolve their attacks, so domain protection strategies need to evolve as well. Learn more from the brand protection experts at Netcraft.

item.title: Fibergrid: Inside the Bulletproof Hosting Network Behind 16,000+ Fake Shops
item.description: Netcraft uncovers Fibergrid, a bulletproof hosting provider enabling 16,000+ fake shops. Learn how stolen IP space, shell companies, and global infrastructure power large-scale fraud, and where it can be disrupted.

item.title: Digital Risk Protection vs Brand Protection
item.description: Learn the key differences between DRPs vs brand protection — and how modern SOCs stop attacks in real time.

item.title: April 2026 Web Server Survey | Netcraft
item.description: In the April 2026 survey we received responses from 1,433,742,238 sites across 299,194,059 domains and 14,198,148 web-facing computers. This reflec...

item.title: Phishing Takedown Speed: Why Time-to-Resolution Matters Most
item.description: Phishing takedown volume shows breadth of visibility. Time-to-resolution shows impact. Learn how to measure whether your program is actively reducing risk.

item.title: Netcraft | The Industry Leader in Enterprise Brand Protection
item.description: Leader in Phishing Detection, Cybercrime Disruption, and Website Takedown

item.title: Camera-First Phishing: How Fraudsters Use Browser Permissions to Harvest Identity Data
item.description: A newly observed phishing campaign impersonates payment verification to harvest selfies, videos, location data, and device information. Learn how camera-first phishing turns browser permissions into a powerful collection channel.

item.title: The Evolution of Phishing: Email > Social Media Impersonation
item.description: Social media impersonation is reshaping phishing. Learn why attackers are shifting beyond email and how security teams can improve phishing detection.

item.title: June 2026 Web Server Survey | Netcraft
item.description: In the June 2026 survey we received responses from 1,489,396,284 sites across 304,146,307 domains and 14,653,771 web-facing computers. This reflect...

item.title: Consumer Scam Reports Show Who’s Fighting Back
item.description: Scam loss reports from Singapore, Australia, and the U.S. reveal a clear divide: countries pairing fraud statistics with active controls are better equipped to disrupt scams.

item.title: Active Defense Blueprint: 2026 Digital Risk Strategy
item.description: Learn how to reduce exposure windows and boost cyber resilience with a 4-layer active defense framework for 2026 digital risk management.

item.title: 6 Best Anti-Phishing Software in 2026
item.description: Explore the top anti-phishing tools for 2026, the capabilities that matter, and how leading solutions compare across detection, visibility, and disruption.

item.title: Booking DOT com Lookalike Pages Hosted on Webflow Subdomains
item.description: Netcraft uncovered Booking.com impersonation pages hosted on Webflow subdomains, leveraging SEO poisoning, lookalike domains, and static phishing infrastructure to target travel-related searches.

item.title: Why DMCA Takedowns Don’t Stop Phishing
item.description: Traditional DMCA takedowns move too slowly to stop AI-generated phishing. Learn why technical disruption closes the victimization window faster.

item.title: Core Components of a Digital Risk Protection Program
item.description: Learn what a digital risk protection program covers, how it works, and how it differs from internal security and fraud tools.

item.title: World Cup Scams Reveal a Dangerous Domain Protection Gap
item.description: Thousands of fake World Cup domains were registered months before kickoff. Learn how domain aging helps attackers build trust before phishing sites go live.

item.title: The 72-Hour Problem with DMCA Takedowns
item.description: Phishing attacks succeed in hours, but DMCA workflows can take days. Explore the operational risks of takedown delays and why security teams need faster disruption methods.

item.title: 2026 World Cup Scams: How Threat Actors Are Exploiting FIFA Fans
item.description: Netcraft research reveals how threat actors are exploiting the 2026 FIFA World Cup through fake ticket sales, betting scams, phishing, and fraudulent hospitality sites.

item.description: Leader in Phishing Detection, Cybercrime Disruption, and Website Takedown
